dataset.description: The Telco customer churn dataset contains information about a fictional telco company that provided home phone and Internet services to 7043 customers in California in Q3. It indicates which customers have left, stayed, or signed up for their service. Multiple important demographics are included for each customer, as well as a Satisfaction Score, Churn Score, and Customer Lifetime Value (CLTV) index. It is designed to facilitate analysis of customer behavior and retention strategies.

desc: The Telco customer churn dataset contains information about a fictional telco company that provided home phone and Internet services to 7043 customers in California in Q3. It indicates which customers have left, stayed, or signed up for their service. Multiple important demographics are included for each customer, as well as a Satisfaction Score, Churn Score, and Customer Lifetime Value (CLTV) index. It is designed to facilitate analysis of customer behavior and retention strategies.
goal: Analyze the impact of different customer demographic factors (e.g., age, gender, income) on churn to identify which factors are most strongly associated with customer churn, helping the company understand the underlying reasons for churn.
name: Visual Analytics System telco_goal1_1742639395

plan.path: The user browses View 1 at the [Overview] level and learns about the overall distribution of demographic categories and churn rates. Next, the user filters gender-specific data through View 1, impacting View 2. In View 2 at the [Exploration] level, the user observes churn scores and selects specific age ranges for detailed analysis in View 3. In View 3, the user analyzes churn rates across age groups with satisfaction scores. Subsequently, View 4 presents detailed proportions of churn categories for senior citizens and individuals with dependents. Finally, View 5 at the [Insight] level visualizes churn rates across cities and age groups, revealing regional patterns or anomalies.

views.components: Add a dropdown to sort bars by gender, age, or other demographic factors.

effect.description: Filter gender-specific data for further analysis in subsequent views.

visualization.description: A bar chart comparing churn rates across demographic categories such as gender.

effect.description: Focus on specific age ranges with high churn scores for detailed analysis.

visualization.description: A scatter plot showing churn scores against age, with gender encoded by color.
An area chart showing churn rates across age groups, segmented by satisfaction scores.

effect.description: Highlight specific churn categories to see proportions in tooltip.

visualization.description: Pie charts displaying churn categories for senior citizens versus others, and customers with dependents versus without.
Heatmap depicting churn rates by city and age group.

data.reason: Linking churn outcomes with demographic attributes is essential to identify correlations and associations.
data.requirement: Combine the churn-related attributes ('churn_label', 'churn_value', 'churn_score', and 'churn_category') with customer demographic attributes ('gender', 'age', 'senior_citizen', 'partner', 'dependents', and 'number_of_dependents') from the 'Customer_Info' table.

data.reason: Aggregated data allows for high-level insights into how demographic groups differ in churn likelihood.
data.requirement: Aggregate churn statistics (e.g., % churned, churn score mean) for each demographic attribute, such as gender, age groups, or senior citizen status.

data.reason: Grouping continuous variables like age enhances interpretability and enables comparison across segments.
data.requirement: Create age groups (e.g., 18-30, 31-45, 46-60, 61+), and compute derived metrics such as churn rates within each age group.

data.reason: Satisfaction may act as a key mediator or explanatory factor behind churn within specific demographic segments.
data.requirement: Incorporate satisfaction scores ('satisfaction_score') from the 'Status_Analysis' table to see how satisfaction moderates the relationship between demographics and churn.

data.reason: Understanding churn reasons by demographic segment can help pinpoint the drivers of churn for specific customer types.
data.requirement: Integrate binary demographic variables (e.g., 'partner', 'dependents') with churn categories to identify patterns in churn reasons.

data.reason: Location-based insights might reveal patterns linked to regional factors like competition or market saturation.
data.requirement: Compare churn metrics (e.g., churn rates, churn scores) between customers with similar demographics in different cities or regions within California.

data.reason: Focusing analyses on high-risk groups allows for targeted investigations and actionable insights.
data.requirement: Filter the data to focus on high-churn categories or high-risk groups, such as senior citizens or customers with dependents.

data.reason: Analyzing churn reasons per demographic group provides qualitative insights into underlying causes.
data.requirement: Link demographic factors with 'churn_reason' textual data for customers who provided a reason for churn, summarizing dominant themes per demographic group.

interaction.reason: Filtering helps users drill down to understand patterns in specific customer subgroups.
interaction.requirement: Enable filtering interactions to isolate a specific demographic group (e.g., filter by gender, age group, or partner status).

interaction.reason: Hover interactions improve detail retrieval while keeping the primary visualization clean.
interaction.requirement: Allow hovering over chart elements to display detailed information (e.g., exact churn score, number of customers, churn reason percentage).

interaction.reason: Brushing supports dynamic exploration of multi-faceted relationships between demographic factors and churn.
interaction.requirement: Enable brushing to select multiple demographic groups (e.g., overlap of age groups and partner status) and update other views accordingly.

interaction.reason: Sorting lets users prioritize insights and examine categories in the order of impact or relevance.
interaction.requirement: Support sorting interactions to reorder categorical axes (e.g., sort churn reasons by frequency, or satisfaction score by age).

interaction.reason: Provides flexibility in exploring relationships and ensures visualizations adapt to different questions.
interaction.requirement: Enable users to toggle between grouped and stacked views in bar charts (e.g., grouping by demographic category or stacking churn/no-churn outcomes).

interaction.reason: Zooming enhances detailed exploration without overwhelming the user by showing all data at once.
interaction.requirement: Allow zooming interactions in scatter plots and heat maps to focus on specific ranges of age or churn scores.

interaction.reason: Switching between raw and normalized views provides new perspectives on the data and highlights proportional insights.
interaction.requirement: Enable users to switch between absolute and relative values in charts, such as raw churn counts vs. percentages.

interaction.reason: Interacting with specific reasons enables targeted analysis to identify demographic patterns driving specific churn categories.
interaction.requirement: Allow users to click on specific churn reasons in tick charts or pie charts to filter the data by the selected reason.

visualization.reason: Bar charts are effective for visualizing and comparing proportions across categorical variables.
visualization.requirement: Use bar charts to compare churn rates across demographic categories (e.g., gender, partner status, senior citizen).

visualization.reason: Scatter plots reveal correlations between churn risk and continuous variables like age and facilitate exploration of gender differences.
visualization.requirement: Create a scatter plot of churn score against age, with gender as a color encoding, and add trend lines.

visualization.reason: Box plots are ideal for examining distributions and identifying outliers or trends within groups.
visualization.requirement: Use box plots to show the distribution of churn scores within demographic groups (e.g., senior citizens, dependents).

visualization.reason: Heat maps help identify high- or low-churn intersections of multiple demographic factors.
visualization.requirement: Create a heat map with churn rate as the colored value and demographic factors (e.g., age groups, partner status) as the axes.

visualization.reason: Pie charts provide an intuitive way to understand categorical breakdowns within demographic strata.
visualization.requirement: Use pie charts to show the proportion of churn categories (e.g., 'Competitor', 'Price') for specific demographic groups.

visualization.reason: Area charts can illustrate trends over grouped variables like age, segmented by another factor such as satisfaction.
visualization.requirement: Design area charts to show changes in churn rates across age groups for different satisfaction scores.

visualization.reason: Overlaying metrics highlights correlations or discrepancies between churn trends and satisfaction levels geographically.
visualization.requirement: Combine a bar chart and a line chart to compare churn rates and satisfaction scores across regions or cities.

visualization.reason: Tick charts can visualize textual or categorical data effectively while preserving ordering for clearer comparisons.
visualization.requirement: Display a tick chart for churn reasons across demographic segments, ordered by frequency.

sqls.SQL: CREATE VIEW temp_view_1 AS SELECT customer_id, gender, age, senior_citizen, partner, dependents FROM Customer_Info
sqls.name: temp_view_1

sqls.SQL: CREATE VIEW temp_view_2 AS SELECT customer_id, churn_label FROM Status_Analysis
sqls.name: temp_view_2

sqls.SQL: CREATE VIEW temp_view_3 AS SELECT customer_id, churn_score FROM Status_Analysis
sqls.name: temp_view_3

sqls.SQL: CREATE VIEW temp_view_4 AS SELECT customer_id, age, gender FROM Customer_Info
sqls.name: temp_view_4

sqls.SQL: CREATE VIEW temp_view_5 AS SELECT customer_id, churn_category FROM Status_Analysis
sqls.name: temp_view_5

sqls.SQL: CREATE VIEW temp_view_6 AS SELECT customer_id, senior_citizen, dependents FROM Customer_Info
sqls.name: temp_view_6

sqls.SQL: CREATE VIEW temp_view_7 AS SELECT customer_id, city FROM Location_Data
sqls.name: temp_view_7

tasks.method: Use bar charts (V1) to compare churn rates across demographic categories like gender, senior citizen status, and partner status. Encode demographic categories on the x-axis and churn rates on the y-axis.
tasks.objective: Establish baseline relationships between categorical demographic factors and churn rates.

tasks.method: Create a scatter plot (V2) of churn scores against age with gender as a color encoding. Add trend lines to highlight correlations.
tasks.objective: Identify how churn risk varies across age and explore differences by gender.

tasks.method: Group ages into bins (e.g., 18-30, 31-45, 46-60, 61+) as specified in D3. Use an area chart (V6) to show changes in churn rates across age groups, segmented by satisfaction scores.
tasks.objective: Identify patterns in churn rates across age groups while exploring the moderating effect of satisfaction.

tasks.method: Use pie charts (V5) to display the proportion of churn categories for senior citizens compared to non-senior citizens and customers with dependents compared to those without.
tasks.objective: Explore whether specific churn reasons dominate for high-risk groups like senior citizens and those with dependents.

tasks.method: Create a heat map (V4) with churn rates as the colored value and demographic factors (e.g., age groups, partner status) on one axis and cities on the other axis.
tasks.objective: Identify regions or demographic intersections with unusually high churn rates.

tasks.method: Use box plots (V3) to show the distribution of churn scores within specific demographic groups (e.g., gender, partner status). Encode churn scores on the y-axis and demographic categories on the x-axis.
tasks.objective: Characterize churn risk distributions within specific demographic groups and detect outliers.

tasks.method: Display a tick chart (V8) to highlight the frequency of churn reasons across gender groups. Allow interactions to filter by gender or reason to dynamically update related visualizations.
tasks.objective: Summarize the most common churn reasons for each gender and enable further exploration.
tasks.type: retrieving values | filtering | summarizing text data

tasks.method: Combine a bar chart and line chart (V7), with churn rates represented as bars and satisfaction scores represented as lines for different cities.
tasks.objective: Highlight whether satisfaction levels correlate with churn rates across different locations.

tasks.method: Allow sorting of a bar chart (V1) to reorder demographic factors by churn rates. Overlay churn categories as segments within the bars for additional context.
tasks.objective: Explore the impact of dependents on churn rates and investigate the reasons behind their churn.

tasks.method: Enable zooming interactions (I6) within scatter plots (V2) to focus on specific age windows with high churn scores.
tasks.objective: Analyze churn risk among high-scoring segments more closely and identify deviations or sub-patterns.
